candidates.studentStem: A:res80->54,miljö70->76,arter18->24. B:res80->83,miljö70->70,arter18->18. C:res80->54,miljö70->52,arter18->12. D:res80->83,miljö70->52,arter18->12. Vilken rad visar resursminskning med biologisk kostnad?
candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: C combines resource 80 to 54 with habitat 70 to 52 and species 18 to 12, so it is resource reduction with biological cost. A is resource reduction with biological gain, B is rising resources with flat biology and D is rising resources with biological cost. (b) Distractor types: A is Type I positive-biology shortcut, B is Type II flat-biology distractor, D is Type III resource-direction mismatch. (c) Cognitive dimensions: two-direction comparison, habitat interpretation, species interpretation and distractor elimination. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests trade-off classification in sustainable production. (e) Knowledge link map: resource direction plus habitat and species direction. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: the direction is specified so B is no longer an alternate opposite trade-off; neutral option labels avoid keyword echo.

shortcut_check.reviewerNote: Pure text format is allowed here because the stem contains compact scenario or numeric data; the answer requires multiple inferences.

candidates.studentStem: A:v1 res80->54,arter18->24; v2 res80->70,arter18->19. B:v1 res80->70,arter18->24; v2 res80->54,arter18->19. C:v1 res80->54,arter18->19; v2 res80->70,arter18->24. D:v1 res80->70,arter18->19; v2 res80->54,arter18->24. Vilken rad ger bäst helhetsstöd för v1?
candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: A gives product one resource 80 to 54 versus product two resource 80 to 70, and product one species 18 to 24 versus product two species 18 to 19. B keeps the species advantage but reverses resource direction, C keeps resource direction but reverses species direction, and D reverses both. Correct A is the only row with lower resource value and higher species value for product one. (b) Distractor types: B is Type I species-only shortcut, C is Type II resource-only shortcut, D is Type III double-reversal. (c) Cognitive dimensions: paired data comparison, resource-pressure reading, species-outcome reading and distractor elimination. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests biological product comparison from two measures. (e) Knowledge link map: resource direction plus species direction. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: the question asks for overall support, not direct word matching; neutral row labels and separated numeric values reduce shortcut risk.

candidates.studentStem: A: åtg+6 ha, sårb70->48, arter18->22. B: åtg+6 ha, sårb70->68, arter18->18. C: åtg+2 ha, sårb70->70, arter18->22. D: åtg+1 ha, sårb70->48, arter18->18. Vilken rad ger starkast stöd för effekt av åtgärden?
candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: A has action plus 6 ha, vulnerability 70 to 48 and species 18 to 22, so it gives the strongest evidence for an effect of the measure. B has the same action area but weak vulnerability change and flat species, C has a smaller action with species gain but flat vulnerability, and D has vulnerability reduction with a much smaller action and flat species. Correct A is the strongest combined effect row. (b) Distractor types: B is Type I action-area shortcut, C is Type II species-only shortcut, D is Type III vulnerability-only shortcut. (c) Cognitive dimensions: action-effect reasoning, vulnerability interpretation, species outcome interpretation, evidence integration and distractor elimination. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests evaluating whether action data and biological outcome data support a climate-adaptation effect. (e) Knowledge link map: action strength plus vulnerability direction plus species direction. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: every row has complete data fields; no answer can be selected from absent-data wording, and the answer requires integrating action size, vulnerability change and species change.
